candidates.stmt: I'm a dedicated Year 6 primary school teacher and a community event organiser, standing to represent Barkham and Arborfield. Through my work in education I support children and families every day, using strong listening, leadership, and problem-solving skills. I also organise Indian cultural events that bring residents together and strengthen community bonds. I am passionate about education, inclusion, and strong local services, and I am committed to serving residents and helping our community remain welcoming, safe, and connected.

I’m proud to stand for Arborfield and Barkham ward, where I’m living for the last 6 years. A community where families, young people, and nature all deserve to thrive.

As someone passionate about protecting our environment and supporting future generations, I want to help create a safer, greener, and more accessible place for everyone, especially our pupils.

If elected as your Green Councillor, I will focus on:

**A greener future for our children:**
– Protecting local green spaces from overdevelopment
– Planting more trees and creating wildlife-friendly areas.
– Expanding no-mow verges and wildflower spaces near schools
– Reducing waste and boosting recycling across the ward

**Safer streets for pupils and families:**
– Introducing 20mph zones around schools and residential areas
– Creating safer crossings and better walking routes to school
– Supporting traffic-free or low-traffic zones at school drop-off times
– Tackling air pollution to ensure cleaner, healthier journeys

**Better, accessible transport:**
– Improving cycle lanes and safe bike storage for students
– Pushing for more reliable and affordable public transport
– Expanding electric vehicle charging while prioritising active travel

**Supporting wellbeing and opportunity:**
– Championing mental health support for young people
– Ensuring safe, inclusive community spaces for all ages
– Advocating for fair funding for local services and schools

Together, we can build an Arborfield and Barkham that puts people, planet, and future generations first.

I have been a proud resident of Wokingham Borough since 2005. My children have grown up here, attended local schools, and enjoyed our beautiful parks. Through door-to-door conversations, I have developed a deep understanding of the issues that matter most to the residents of Bulmershe and Coronation ward.

1. Parking Fee Adjustments: o   Advocate for the repeal of recent increments in parking fees. o   Reinstate complimentary parking on Sundays and evenings to invigorate local economic vitality and bolster businesses.

2. Sanitation Management: o   Uphold weekly refuse collection schedules to ensure communal satisfaction and ecological purity. o   Prevent fly-tipping, which exacts considerable financial and environmental tolls.

3. Primary and Secondary School Education o   Number of school places need to be increased. o   Work thoroughly to increase extra-curricular and sports activities for young people.

 4. Community Safety Enhancement: o   Intensify collaborations with law enforcement to efficaciously address anti-social behaviour and reduce incidences of shoplifting. o   Implement regulatory measures for electric scooters to safeguard pedestrian safety.

5. Infrastructure Investment: o   Champion augmented allocations for the road maintenance budget within a Conservative council framework. o   Prioritize the safety and maintenance of infrastructure, responding to resident concerns.

6. Sustainable Development and Environmental Stewardship: o   Commit to the conservation of green areas to strike an equilibrium between urban development and ecological preservation. o   Acknowledge the enduring advantages of sustainable land management on property valuations and communal well-being.

I hold a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and have completed the Oxford University Executive Leadership Programme. Currently, I serve as a Human Resources Director in the telecommunications sector and am a member of the PBBC Business Council, where I support local businesses an

I've lived near Woodley for over 20 years. I worked as an accountant and for local charities, before I recently retired. Now I volunteer on the till of the Oxfam bookshop in Woodley on Saturday mornings, am a churchwarden at Earley St Peter's and have been a member of Earley Town Council for the last 3 years.

I'm interested in the environment, transport, fair trade, and the eradication of poverty locally, nationally and around the world. I joined the Liberal Democrats because I feel the two main parties are out of touch with local issues and don't care about local people and their concerns. I also realised that just voting in elections didn't change much so I decided to get involved in local politics and try to make a difference.

I live in Emmbrook, where my children attend a local school, and have called it home since 2019. I've been a Councillor for two years and am standing for re-election in May, to continue helping you make the most of living in this fantastic area. I work in management for a global technology company and proudly serve on town and borough committees with the Lib Dem team.

I'm standing for election because I care deeply about Emmbrook and the people who live here. I want to use my professional background in a global company to bring strong leadership, accountability, and fresh thinking to local decision-making. As part of the Lib Dem team, I will keep standing up for Emmbrook and helping our community thrive.

I’ve proudly called Wokingham home since 2020, after previously spending many years working in government service in the UK and overseas. Originally from London, I chose Wokingham for its strong community, character, and quality of life. Having seen how poor decisions and weak governance can damage communities, I am determined to help protect what makes our town special while ensuring services improve for all residents.

If elected, I will work hard to represent our community and deliver meaningful results. My priorities include protecting green spaces and preventing overdevelopment, cutting council financial waste and holding it to account on residents’ behalf, supporting local businesses with measures such as one hour of free parking, delivering promised school investment, and ensuring residents have a stronger voice on the issues that matter.

Wokingham deserves responsible leadership that safeguards its future, and I look forward to serving our community in this capacity should you choose to vote for me.

I have worked in Wokingham for fifteen years and a decade ago knew it was the place I wanted to raise my family in. I want to use my experience representing colleagues at company, industry and national level as a union representative to push for the best outcomes for local people. I’ve seen with you many changes in the last ten years and would not suggest for a second that we should stand still however now is the time we need to put in the work. The work to maintain our welcoming community, the work to transition the borough to clean energy and the work to protect the natural beauty we’re lucky to live in.

I'm a local family woman, mother of two, and have lived in the area for nearly 20 years. Having raised my children here, I have first-hand insight into the issues affecting local families. As a teacher in local primary schools and a tutor, I understand families’ daily challenges. Empathetic and committed to listening, I value fairness and equality. My interests in birdwatching, dance, music, and art keep me connected to local cultural groups. I'm standing for councillor to give back and ensure every voice is heard.

Raising my children here has shown me the care and support our community offers. This motivated me to stand for election, give back, and ensure every voice is heard and valued.

I moved to Wokingham 18 months ago for a graduate job, and I have never felt more welcomed. I love this area and I am proud to call it my home.

Too often our politics revolve around pessimism and negativity. I joined the Green Party to fight for a brighter future for all of us and advocate for the real solutions to the problems all of us share. My dream is to make this area, this country, and the whole world a fairer, friendlier, and greener place to live.

I have lived in Finchampstead for over 10 years and in that time, I have come to appreciate the rural environment, the wildlife and the friendly people.

It was to help protect the unique environment in Finchampstead that I stood for election 4 years ago and have demonstrated that I am willing to put in the time and effort to ensure we preserve this special area that we call home.

Two years ago, I fought to stop a massive care home being built amongst the residential area on Lower Wokingham Road and I pushed for the Longwater Lane development to be refused.

I have run a campaign to prevent Church Farm being turned into a housing estate and I have worked closely with the Parish Council to improve the old War Memorial junction to reduce the number of accidents.

I will also work hard to ensure Rooks Nest Farm and the delayed Barkham Solar Farm are not developed for housing.

I'm originally from France and have lived in Finchampstead with my wife and two daughters for 19 years. I've worked in high-tech companies, and volunteered at Gorse Ride Schools as a parent governor. I want to make Finchampstead a thriving community and it would be an honour to serve as a Borough Councillor. I'll listen to all residents to understand what matters to them, and help make a real difference in Finchampstead. Our community has experienced many changes over the years, with pressures from development and increased traffic. I want to ensure that local residents have a voice.

I am asking for your support for the next four years.
My family and I have lived in Finchampstead for the past 6 years and in the local area since 2017.  Given I am putting myself forward as a candidate to improve Finchampstead and Wokingham, should I be elected, I will donate 100% of my councillor’s allowance to local charities in the Finchampstead area.
What I Will Fight For
•	Local people first for housing — if you’ve been on the waiting list in Finchampstead, you should be prioritised over people moving into the area. Our residents have paid into the system and they should come first.
•	Your community, your say — major decisions that affect Finchampstead should involve local residents. Whether it’s the Borough becoming a preferred destination for asylum seekers, significant developments, or changes to local services — you should have a say before decisions are made, not after.
•	Education that delivers — aiming for high standards in our infant, junior, primary and secondary schools. Making sure there are enough places, enough teachers, and enough teaching assistants.
•	Reform UK is the only party serious about changing direction. Locally, I want to bring the same Reform UK commonsense, no-nonsense approach to Wokingham Borough Council.
It Is Your Choice
Thank you for taking the time to read this. If you have any questions or local issues you’d like to raise, I would genuinely welcome hearing from you.

Born in RBH and raised in Woodley, I moved to the Finchampstead area in 2010 and while my children attended Gorse Ride School I was Chair of the PTA, raising £20k each year I was in office. In 2020 I received a Civic Award for my community work running a youth club at the FBC Centre with my husband, Martin and volunteering for Finchampstead based domestic abuse charity, Kaleidoscopic UK.

Having been a Link visitor with Link Visiting Scheme for a decade, I set up the Friendship Table that meets weekly for isolated, older people and ran the FBC community team as a volunteer for 7 years for which I was awarded the Platinum Jubilee Award by Finchampstead Parish Council. During this period I set up the local Share Wokingham site that runs at the FBC every Tuesday; ran a Welcome course for HongKongers new to the community and organised sports sessions for refugees. Standing to represent Finchampstead seems a natural next step in serving the community.

We've lived in Lower Earley for nearly 26 years, and our children were educated at local schools. I worked in a large secondary school for 22 years and have always been interested in young people's needs, as well as meeting all members of our community. My family and I love living in Lower Earley with its green spaces, and the closeness of local amenities make it an ideal place for everyone.

I've thoroughly enjoyed meeting people and discussing local issues with them in my year as a Wokingham Borough Councillor, and look forward to continuing this. I'm very proud of what I've achieved in the year since l was elected, including being elected as Deputy Mayor for Earley Town Council in May 2025 and dealing with a range of issues from local residents including:

Helping two residents regarding school placement issues 
Dealing with tree issues 
Highlighting parking issues opposite Hawkendon School
Helping residents with housing issue

Michael is an experienced and qualified insurance professional of over 40 years and who currently works in Henley-on-Thames for a well known insurance broker.  He is an experienced Councillor on Wokingham Borough Council.  Since 2019 he has been the Councillor for Sonning & Warren.

He campaigns on local issues and helping local residents, such as working for safer roads by managing traffic flow and reducing speeding/dangerous driving. Also, protecting the Green Belt and the environment, such as the pollution of the River Thames.  By speaking out and making representations against inappropriate development.

It will be great honour for Michael to be elected as one of the Councillors for the new Thames Ward on Wokingham Borough Council.  If so, he will do his best to represent and action your views and concerns.  Also, to keep residents who raise issues fully updated.

He was born in Hampshire, but has lived most of his life in Earley where he was educated locally at Earley St Peter’s Primary School and then Maiden Erlegh Secondary School. In his spare time he is interested in football, cricket, snooker, and keen on gardening, drawing and painting.

I care deeply about the future of our community. I work in wellbeing and spend much of my time outdoors in nature, which has given me a direct appreciation for both people’s health and the environment we depend on.
I’m standing with the Green Party because it is the only party not funded by billionaires and big corporate interests, and the only party putting the interests of working people at the centre of its vision. I believe we need honest politics that prioritises people, community, and the environment over short-term profit.
My focus is on protecting green spaces, supporting wellbeing, and building a fairer, more sustainable future for everyone.

I've been a Hillside Borough Councillor for nearly 7 years, and am also a Town Councillor and have served as the Mayor of both Wokingham Borough and Earley Town. I lived in Lower Earley for 42 years, attended the University of Reading as a mature student and my three children all went to local schools. I worked for 23 years before retirement for a national charity.

I believe national government, regardless of party, have failed to understand and address the realities of everyday lives. Locally, I've seen what damage twenty years of Tory leadership has done, and I'm proud about how hard the Liberal Democrats are working to create a fair and inclusive community for all.

Nick has lived in Winnersh since 2012. Starting his career in theatre, he later moved into corporate role, including opening Imax theatres globally. In 2009, he founded the Cratus Group, a communication agency focused on local planning and public affairs. Nick served as a Councillor and is now a Parish Councillor. His son was educated at Winnersh Primary and Emmbrook School and they are both season ticket holders for Reading FC. Nick is also a guest writer and podcast host on local government topics and has been hosting the annual Chamberlain Lecture since its launch. Nick has the expertise to help Winnersh residents on local issues. Nick is opposed to the LibDem plans to build 3,900+ houses at Hall Farm and the damaging impact from thousands of additional cars on local roads, such as Hatch Farm Way and Mole Road. School places are a priority for Nick. We must have enough school places for local children, including the addition provision for special educational needs. He will take action to tackle local flooding issues, help residents on new estates deal with housing management companies, and campaign for a new state-of-the-art Royal Berkshire Hospital. Nick wants to see an improvement in the quality of our roads and transport links. And stop the LibDems war on local motorists. They have doubled parking charges and plan to waste £millions to redesign the Woosehill roundabout, causing increased congestion on an already busy road.

Russell is 48 years old and has lived in the Wokingham constituency for 26 of the last 33 years and was in neighboring Reading for the rest of the time. He has joined the Green Party more than 15 years ago and been active in the Reading and Wokingham Party for much of that time. He has stood in local elections. A long-time academic, Russell has a first degree in Ecology, a Masters degree in Environmental Sciences and a PhD in Biodiversity Management, specialising in giraffe conservation. He is now a Sustainability Manager working to reduce the impacts of the sports industry.
Russell's approach recognises the need for economic progress while taking into account very real and often damaging environmental and social impacts. In reality our economic system is an element of and sits within our broader social system, while human society relies completely on the environment and the ecosystem services that support all life and natural processes. If elected Russell will brig this pragmatic approach to bear on local, national and international issues. Only the Green Party holds the environment as central to human happiness and prosperity, and only the Green Party can hope to address the impending environmental crises that we face today.

I first moved to Woodley in 1959 when my parents moved from London, and was a pupil at The Forest School until moving on to Reading College to study Business Studies.     After qualifying from Reading Technical College, I enjoyed a long career in Finance before a career change into Hospitality.   In 2002 I moved to France where I taught basic English to Primary School children , providing a grounding in English before they progressed to secondary education.  In 2006 I returned to care for my elderly parents and have lived and worked in the community ever since.  In 2018 I was elected to the Town Council, and have served on the Planning and Community Committee and until May 2023 was Chair of the Leisure Services Committee together with many working parties.  I was also a Trustee with Citizens Advice, Wokingham for almost 4 years.   I respectfully, therefore, ask for your support to elect me as one of your Borough Councillors so that I can represent the interests of residents in Loddon Ward and help get value from the services we all rightfully deserve from Wokingham Borough Council

I was born and raised in Wokingham and have lived across the borough for 20+ years. I feel lucky to have grown up here, and care deeply about our communities and our people.

Politics has stopped listening to us, and I’m standing to change that. We deserve a system that works for us, not shareholders. I’ll always speak up and say what needs to be said, to make sure your voice is heard.

I’d be honoured to earn your support. You’ll see me around Loddon, ready to listen and stand with you. Together we can make honesty & hope normal again.

My wife and I moved to Woodley over 25 years ago to bring up our three children. They all attended local schools (Highwood, Bulmershe and Waingels). We found that Woodley has a great sense of community, some lovely open spaces, and is a lovely place to raise a family.

Professionally I’m a Chartered Electrical Engineer. I work in the process and new energy sectors (Battery factories, carbon capture schemes, hydrogen plants) I’ve been fortunate that my career has taken me around the world and I’ve been assigned to offices in the US and Italy.

I’m an Elder at Wycliffe Baptist Church (down at Cemetery Junction) where I lead worship and occasionally preach, and I teach in Sunday school. I serve on the Wokingham SACRE (Standing Advisory Council on Religious Education).

Lib Dems want to see people flourish as individuals by being part of a community. I think more needs to be done to strengthen youth services here. We also need to build more reasonably priced housing for young people as they start in life. My children have had to move away to find somewhere affordable.

I'm currently a Councillor in Maiden Erlegh and Whitegates. I was a District Councillor in Hong Kong too, where I worked hard to serve the community and strive for democracy. In 2021 I was forced to resign and leave Hong Kong for safety as Beijing carried out mass arrests of Democratic politicians. I now live in Earley with my family and have a son who attends Maiden Erlegh School.

To serve the community and to strive for fairness have been my aspiration since I was a kid. I enjoy solving people’s problems, making people’s voices heard, improving governance, upholding democratic values, and I volunteer in British Hong Kong people's organisations. I know how important a Councillor is to the local community and how symbolic it is to be the first former Hong Kong District Councillor elected in the UK. Although my mission was suppressed in Hong Kong, I'm honoured to serve the people of Maiden Erlegh and Whitegates and I do hope you will re-elect me in May.

I have lived in the local area with my family since 1988, so I am fully aware of the concerns that impact local residents because your issues are my issues.

I have been self-employed for the past 38 years and am committed to the growth and vitality of our local community.

At the heart of this is my belief that we must enhance the skills of young people and create opportunities for them to be responsive to economic, social, technological, and political changes. The recent pandemic has taught us all to reconsider the way we live, work and travel.

My experience as a borough councillor serving neighbouring Park ward in Reading, from 2007 – 2011, gave me valuable awareness of environment protection, local infrastructure, supporting local businesses, improving education, and tackling anti-social behaviour.

Public service is what drives me. I am committed to helping others and standing up for the residents of Bulmershe and Whitegates.

I can only do this with your support and trust. Please contact me to share your views or if you need any help.

I’ve lived in the ward since 2012, when my wife and I got tired of the Parisian suburb, and decided to move to the UK. We have never regretted this decision, and thoroughly enjoyed living here with our 3 daughters. The youngest is currently at the Bulmershe School.

I’m a software engineer, and work at the ECMWF (European Center for Mid-range Weather Forecast, on Shinfield road). I commute with my bicycle of course: we've lived without a car for 5 years now. I'm part of Kidical Mass Reading, who organises family-friendly bike rides in town, to increase families’ confidence in cycling together, and to highlight the need for better cycling infrastructure.

If elected, I will campaign to reduce the place of cars in the public space, and promote the use of walking, cycling or public transports. Cars are necessary, but the fewer there are, the happier we get! I will also protect and improve the green space, and support our amazing local, independent businesses.  We all have a role to play to fight climate change.  At our level, this is also simply to improve the life of people in the borough!

I have represented Norreys since 2019 on Wokingham Town Council and Wokingham Borough Council.

I have worked hard and dedicated much of my time to Council matters and representing Norreys residents.

I work in NHS mental health services in Hampshire and am on the management committee of a local school.

My particular areas of knowledge and interest are: - health & mental health - social care - education  - project planning

I want to make a difference where I most can.  I have a proud record of raising money for local schools and charities, advocating on behalf of local residents and I live in the heart of Norreys so everything that affects the ward affects me and my family. If you elect me, I promise to be open, available and continue to work hard on behalf of you all.

As your taxes and the cost of living go up, nothing works anymore! I believe that we are on a doom loop which soon will be irretrievable.  That’s why I am standing! I’m writing to ask for your support. If elected, I will donate my councillor’s allowance to charity.

I have lived in the Wokingham area for over sixty years. You may recall that I was the Conservative Leader of the Council through Covid. I stood down because I could no longer support the Tory Government, a party which since 1990, has not been conservative nor unionist.

I am a conservative … I believe in small government, enterprise, ambition, the individual, compassion, low taxation, competence, the rule of law, freedom, personal responsibility, free market economics, fiscal responsibility, sovereignty, and the Union. I am extremely proud to be English, a nation with a long successful history of achievement. England created the world’s modern way of life.

There is not a sliver of difference between Labour, LibDems and the Tories, who collectively have brought a proud, talented, wealthy and powerful nation to its knees – big government, high taxation, national humiliation, weak defence, huge borrowings, curtailment of personal liberty, loss of freedom of speech, a strongly woke agenda, net zero, poor health and education, DEI, abandonment of Ulster, Gibraltar, Chagos, substantial waste, reversal of Brexit, ludicrous net zero policies, ineffective policing and border control, an eroded justice system and worst of all incompetence and mendacity to a level never seen before.  When Starmer said this was a government of service he meant self-service for those living off the taxpayer himself included.

It has been interesting to see much of the good my administration did, being claimed by the current Lib-Dem administration as their initiative. Other than declaring a borough of sanctuary… the current administration’s only success has been to run down reserves.

This is a borough election, locally my focus wil

I've lived in Wokingham for over twenty years and currently live in Woosehill. I spent twenty-eight years in the Insurance industry, and for the last sixteen years, I've worked in the voluntary sector, mainly as a social historian.

In May 2023 I was elected as a Town Councillor for Norreys East and currently I'm Deputy Mayor meeting individuals, businesses and charities to enhance the lives of residents. I think the opinions and concerns of the residents are paramount to anybody who wants to be an effective Councillor, working to make sure that views are heard in decision making processes, whether that's to support an individual's specific needs or to balance the needs of more people around general issues such as illegal parking or speeding. I'm passionate about hearing everybody's views and working to help all residents.

Norreys is full of wonderful people and is the place I call home. We made the decision to move to Wokingham seven years ago because we knew it would be a great place to settle and raise a family.

We all deserve a happy and healthy life. I want to see a kinder, fairer world where everyone has the opportunity to achieve this. That starts with helping my local community.

I believe freedom is only real when you have the means to use it. That's why I'm a social liberal, because opportunity without support is just a slogan, and community without liberty is just control.

Originally from East Sussex, I worked as a geologist in Australia's oil and gas industry before becoming disillusioned with big oil and moving into environmental consultancy as a hydrogeologist. My partner and I moved back to the UK in 2021, settling in Shinfield with our young son. Since then I've stood for the Liberal Democrats in local elections and am a contributor to Liberal Democrat Voice, writing on intergenerational fairness, to date. I want to build a credible, evidence-based case for the reforms our community and country need. And I want Shinfield to have a strong voice on Wokingham Borough Council, someone who puts residents first.

I’m Keiron Titus, standing for councillor in Shinfield. I grew up in Wokingham, went away to university in Southampton and work in London 8, and then moved back a couple of years ago – this time to give my daughter the same thing I had: a community with real opportunity and a wide range of local experiences.

I work in marketing, and on weekends I volunteer as a qualified boxing coach (England Boxing) teaching children and young people. Through sport, I get to help young people build self-confidence, discipline, and a healthy attitude to fitness. I strongly believe in the value of positive role models that come not just from within the immediate family, but from the wider community too. It genuinely takes a village.

I’m standing because I believe the traditional big political parties have failed working people. Local services and good decision-making have been left behind. If elected, I’ll focus on community building, youth development, and safer, greener spaces in Shinfield, working with residents to make sure our area is somewhere families can thrive.

I’d be honoured to earn your support. Let’s keep Shinfield welcoming, supportive, and a great place for young people to grow up.

I’ve lived in Woodley for 60 years, the last 19 years in Kingfisher Drive. I have represented Southlake on the Borough Council for the last two years and am the Borough Mayor. I am a Woodley Town Councillor, but, when not at meetings and talking to residents, I spend time with my grandchildren, on my allotment, and delivering FOCUS leaflets. I am a School Governor, a litter picker with the WASP team and volunteer garden with the Wokingham Volunteer Centre.

As a Southlake Borough and Town Councillor, I know how important it is be involved in my local community, listening to people’s concerns and trying to make a difference.

I have lived in the South Lake Ward since 2010 and also work locally as a primary school teacher. Through my work in the community, I have built strong relationships with many local families and gained a deep understanding of the issues that matter most to residents.

I am committed to helping make our area a clean, safe and fair place to live for everyone. As a parent of two young sons, I am especially motivated to ensure that the neighbourhood they grow up in — and the community we all share — is the best it can be. That is why I am standing as a council candidate: to work with residents and help build a stronger future for South Lake.

As a Borough Councillor for 2 years from May 2022, I became acutely aware of the struggle to balance council finances and the dual issues of inflation and underfunding. I am standing again in order to continue the Liberal Democrats' strong track record of sound financial management and investing in the future of our Borough. My career has been varied, spanning roles in communications and engineering, as well as 25 years in the RAF. Most recently I was a consultant with a software company, working with domestic and foreign governments, security services and banks. I have two adult married children - a son who followed my steps into the RAF and a daughter who is a management accountant - and three grandchildren.

World travel has opened my eyes to the diversity of cultures, the amazing nature we take for granted and all that can be lost for our children and grandchildren if we do not take care of our planet and ourselves. We owe all that follow us to create a safe and varied place for them to grow, explore and achieve all they aspire to be.

I am a Chartered Accountant and businessman who has been living in the west of Wokingham Borough for over 50 years. I first moved to Arborfield in 1968, and then Swallowfield in 1984 were I bought my first house and subsequently moved with my wife to Spencers Wood in 1997 where we have lived for the last 27 years. I was first elected to Wokingham District Council in 1994 serving until 2019. I held several executive roles most notably the finance portfolio where I ensured that the Council’s finance was soundly based leaving the finances in very good order in 2019. I enjoyed my previous roles and want to give back to my community again.

I have seen the many changes that have taken place in the area together with the continuing challenges experienced by residents of Spencers Wood and Swallowfield. Having continued to speak to local people on the doorsteps over recent years about their issues, I feel it is time for me to be involved again. I look forward to helping residents with their problems and working to make Wokingham Borough Council a flagship authority for its service and value for money. I believe that my previous experience on the council together with my financial and business experience will an asset ensuring that the best services will be delivered for residents.

I do hope I get the opportunity to be one of your Borough Councillors’ after 2nd May Elections and look forward to serving you in the years to come. Anthony Pollock

I was born and grew up in Farley Hill and moved back to the parish in 2008 after working 20 years in Africa in international development work. I am passionate about environment and climate issues and have been active in Swallowfield in support of helping set up a community orchard in Riseley and supporting broader conservation initiatives. 

If elected, I aim to work towards better public transport, improve cycling infrastructure, support greater tree planting across the borough and help the most vulnerable with the cost of living crisis

Hello all! I'm Kathryn Allman, standing for Spencers Wood and Swallowfield on 7th May 2026 as the Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition (TUSC) candidate.

I'm a mum to two primary age children, living on Croft Gardens. I'm running because I've struggled, and watched neighbours struggle, to access the basics our community needs: school places, dentists, GPs, referrals, children's mental health support, SEND provision.

Take Alder Grove Primary. This week they announced that the catchment radius for September entry is 0.289 miles. Children are being pushed out of their own community to schools miles away. Not every parent drives, and plenty are juggling multiple drop offs, work and caring responsibilities. The council knew the demographics. The developers knew the demand. Neither built the infrastructure, because it eats into profit margins.

Here's what I'll push for:

- Build the new Spencers Wood primary on Croft Gardens, and make it two form entry instead of one

- ⁠Refuse to sign off new developments until school, GP and SEND provision is in place first, not promised later

- ⁠Address the Shinfield Meadows misallocation. £111m raised was diverted elsewhere, including to Bohunt in Arborfield, which most of our children can't access. Developers should have to provide funds to ensure all children thrive in a school they can walk to. We shouldn’t have to choose which community has accessible and good quality education.

On tax and cuts, I won't choose between the two. I'm not going to vote for council tax rises that punish working families for shortfalls in central government funding, and I'm not going to vote for cuts to services either. That means using the council's reserves and prudential borrowing powers to protect what we have, and demanding government properly fund local councils instead of squeezing residents.

We live in a system set up to fail ordinary people. I don't accept that, and I don't think you should either. We deserve to thrive in our communities

I live in Charvil with my husband and two young children and love the strong community and surrounding countryside.
I've represented Thames Ward since May 2024 and also serve as a Charvil Parish Councillor. After a decade working in the private sector focused on climate finance, I am now a full-time councillor and parent. 
I'm passionate about creating a sustainable future, and have been Wokingham's Executive member for Environment and Climate Emergency since March 2025.

My key local priorities are:
- protecting the environment- both local nature and reducing greenhouse gas emissions;
- improving road safety for all users, especially walkers and cyclists;
- trying to maximise the benefit to the local community of any future development - increasing the proportion of affordable homes and pushing for future-proof design that integrates with our communities.

I’ve lived in the Thames Ward for my entire life, and have spent time in and around almost all of the nature this area has to offer. I’m also, like the other members of this ward, aware of the challenges we face with increasing threats to wildlife and other societal shifts.

The Green Party offers a unique sense of hope in regards to issues faced in both the local area and on a national scale, and I believe that starting small is just the first step forward in shaping our future – both as a community and as a country.

I'm originally from the Netherlands and came to live in the UK in 1988 to study at Reading University and then Imperial College in London. After 14 years working overseas I returned with my family to Twyford in 2015. I have a keen interest in local history, genealogy and archaeology.

I've been a member of the Twyford Parish Council since 2022 and am proud to have been a Councillor for Twyford, Ruscombe and Hurst since May 2024.

I have lived in the area since 2018, and like all who live here I have a love for the villages and their unique blend of community, connectivity, character and countryside.
  
However for years the same issues have been raised, and for years, the same frustrations have gone unanswered.  Our ward is home to the leader of the council, a career Politician who tells of the fights he has had on our behalf for many years.  
However it is not always clear who he is fighting with, since the same issues seem to be present with nothing achieved, and things getting worse in some areas.

Our recent council tax bill for the year, has another increase of 5% from last year.  And in many ways it seems we get less and less for that money.  In this time of rising financial pressures that was another blow many of us could ill afford.  Part of that rise will no doubt be to fund the Twyford Library, which was recently refurbished with a budget of over £1 million.  I like the idea of a Library, however it doesn’t include a public toilet and in the days of the internet would seem to be little more than a vanity project, and I question if it is truely the best use of our money.

Meanwhile we have had at least three major new building developments approved in the ward and with the Council also declaring Wokingham a Borough of Sanctuary will soon place an additional burden on our beautiful area.  This at a time when we are already seeing unprecedented traffic and parking issues, especially near the station, and at Ruscombe cross roads and have frequent reports of shop lifting and unlawful behaviour around our shops.

I am standing because Twyford, Ruscombe and Hurst each deserve stronger, more determined representation — someone who will challenge complacency, push for real improvements, and ensure that our villages get their fair share of investment and attention. We cannot keep accepting slow to no progress while our roads worsen, our services strain, and our village centre faces growing p

My name is Merv Boniface and I have lived in Wokingham for my whole working life. I graduated from Reading University and have my three degrees from there. I live in Twyford and have done so for the past 38 years. I was a local councillor for eight years until 2019. I entered the teaching profession and have always worked in Wokingham schools. The last of these was from 1990 to 2010 when I was appointed Deputy Headteacher at The Bulmershe School in Woodley.
I joined the Green Party in 2020 and have never looked back following that decision. I have always been passionate about Green issues from river and waterway purity, plastic in our seas to energy conservation, animal protection and climate change amongst many others. In fact, I have never felt so at home with Green policies which have come to mean so much to us all. 
I am Chair of Wokingham Green Party and we are making huge strides towards our target of a number of Green councillors at the Borough Council following 7th May 2026 Local Elections.  Hopefully this will happen for Twyford, Ruscombe and Hurst Ward residents. I have stood as a Green candidate on many occasions but now is the time. 
As part of this it is very important that the Green Party hold their own in Parliamentary elections. I was selected by Green Party members to represent you at the July 2024 General Election. I stood to represent you all on the issues that impact on us directly and have profound consequences for our children and future generations.

The Green Party is a fast rising political force that is clear and precise in its intentions and ambitions. We aim to tackle the climate crisis head on, do all we can to improve the environment and ensure social justice for all. We believe in fairness in the provision of public services, conservation of everything around us and financially a chance for everyone to have a comfortable life.

We are the Party for the future and reach out to all generations. Our Wokingham membership has quadrupled in

Hello, I’m Stuart Hooper, and I’m standing to represent Twyford on Wokingham Borough Council.

I’ve lived in and around Twyford and Ruscombe since 2016, and I recently joined Twyford Parish Council. I enjoy volunteering across the borough by cleaning road signs, and litter picking our streets, parks, and rivers.

Over the past 15 years, I’ve been designing public services within the central government, to make improvements in the way services are delivered to you. I intend to bring the skills and knowledge earned in that time to represent Twyford.

I grew up on a small farm near Exmoor, and helping build our family home taught me the value of hard work and taking pride in where you live. Those values guide me today, which is why I settled here in Twyford where the sense of community is strong.

If elected, I will focus on:

1. Keeping parks, waterways, and footpaths safe, clean, and accessible.

2. Ensuring borough-wide developments meet local and environmental needs, while respecting Twyford’s character, though the creation of a citizens climate assembly.

3. Advocating for better commuter transport and smarter traffic planning, focussing on scalable solutions over permanent silver bullets, inline with Wokingham's climate emergency action plan.

4. Supporting high street shops and small businesses to keep Twyford vibrant. Ideally helping find opportunities for young people to start-up or join the workforce.

If you see me around Twyford, or at a local event, please do say hello. I want to hear about your experiences, ideas, and concerns so we can make Twyford and the surrounding areas an even better place to live.

I would really appreciate your support. Please get in touch if you would like to know more.

Stuart Hooper

In my two years as your Borough Councillor, I have served on the Licensing and Children’s Services committees, using my twenty years of teaching experience to advocate for families. Now a Science teacher at Reading Girls' School for the last three years, I am deeply involved in local life, from the Wokingham Horticultural Association to training with Berkshire Search and Rescue Dogs. My priority remains being an accessible representative who champions your concerns and ensures every resident feels heard.

​I am motivated by a passion for public service and accountability. My goal is to be a bridge between residents and the council, ensuring our community remains inclusive, supportive, and heard.

I have lived in the Wescott Ward with my family for over 35 years. Our children grew up here and all went to local schools. If elected I would work on behalf of all residents and measure all services by how compatible they were with social justice and the view that planet Earth is a living, self-regulating organism and needs our cooperation.

I'm currently a Borough Councillor where I'm a member of Childcare Services, the Corporate Parenting Board,  and the Climate Emergency and Audit Committees. I'm also a Wokingham Town Councillor, and a member of the Planning and Transportation and Arts and Culture committees.

I've lived in Winnersh for more than 20 years and work as a senior business analyst in the Finance Services sector. I volunteer for charities helping people with mental health challenges and help organise mental health awareness workshops for people in Wokingham. I want to help create harmony, respect, love, unity and peace for the various communities living in Wokingham and I'm a member of the interfaith forum of a Wokingham Charity. I have immense love for Winnersh and want to ensure that it remains a green, clean, secure area for families to flourish and feel safe during the day and at night.

I moved here in 1998 to study at Reading University and I liked the area so much I have lived here ever since. Within ten minutes walk of Asda for most of that time! After completing my PhD in Air Pollution I embarked on a career in transport planning and have worked as an analyst and consultant for councils and companies.

I'm passionate about making trains and buses more accessible, reliable and economically attractive to encourage more people to leave their car at home saving money and reducing congestion. I regularly choose to use the bus as a greener and cheaper mode of transport Luckily, I often work from home, which means I can enjoy our beautiful area or tend my garden. I'm an active member of Shinfield Players Theatre, you may have seen me in Snow White back in January. 

As a keen swimmer, I enjoy clocking up lengths at Loddon Valley and the new Bulmershe Leisure Centre. Now you know more about me, I'd like to hear from you. I have enjoyed many doorstep discussions and hope to meet you soon. 

However, don't wait for my knock! Please contact me to share your views and if you see me out and about, even in Asda or at a bus stop, please say hello and have a chat. 

I want to work with Cllr Pauline Jorgensen to ensure Hillside continues to be a great place to live. With your support, I can do that.

I’ve lived in Winnersh for the past decade and am raising two primary school-age children here. Like many local families, I rely on our roads, schools, and community services every day. I value our green spaces and believe they are a vital part of what makes Winnersh such a great place to live. After 20 years working in corporate roles, I want to bring my experience and a practical mindset to helping build an even stronger community for everyone who lives here.
I have lived in Wokingham Borough since the mid 90s and have seen my family grow up in our town. I am an active member of Wokingham Baptist Church, and a Trustee of the Free Church Burial Ground.

Seòna Turtle grew up and still lives in Wokingham Without. She ran Sebastian's Girl Guides for 15 years and has served as a Wokingham Without Cllr for 25 years.

During her time as Chairman she improved and increased the outdoor facilities in Pinewood including: the installation of 2 popular play parks, a viewing platform over the pond with a picnic area nearby, regenerating and opening the orchard, and tarmacking the gravel the carpark. She successfully campaigned for a “refuge island” on Nine Mile Ride enabling safer pedestrian access into Pinewood.

As well as a Parish Councillor, Seòna is a trustee of Circle Hill and Walter Recreation ground, a member of Friends of Heathlake and Chairman of Pinewood Community Orchard.

As a long standing resident Seòna will use her vast experience and knowledge of the area to represent the needs of our community, and to ensure it remains a pleasant and safe place, in which to live, now and in the future.

I've lived in Wokingham for over 23 years, the last 16 years in Wokingham Without, where with my husband I've raised two children, both of whom attended our village schools. I'm passionate about our community and love walking my rescue dog Freya around Heath Lake, Buckler’s Park and Crowthorne Woods.

I work full time, but enjoy carving out time to volunteer across different areas. I was motivated to get involved in local politics because I wanted to understand how local policy and decisions that have a direct impact on people’s everyday lives are made. I care about our community and would like to play an active role in shaping its future, helping make sure decisions reflect the real needs, concerns, and aspirations of local residents.

I believe in fighting divisive politics and working to make Wokingham Without the truly great place it is to live and work in. I've lived in the Wokingham area since 1990 and am currently living in Wokingham Without. My background in construction and sustainability brings insights into issues such as planning, Climate Change and Energy and sustainable development.

When I'm not annoying my friends and family, I'm always looking for things to do to keep out of their way. I'm a passionate traveller who doesn’t speak at least four languages, but I do enjoy using Duolingo and other translation apps.

David moved to Wokingham in 2009 and has been very active in our community ever since. A passionate campaigner David served as member and latterly leader of Wokingham Town Council from 2011 to 2019 and was elected as Borough Councillor for Wokingham Without in May 2022.

Since his election he has fought against inappropriate development in the ward, delivered new road safety schemes, pressed utilities to plan better including starting engagement with Bracknell Council, and seen real improvement in the roads such as the resurfacing of Nine Mile Ride, whilst there is a lot still to be done David’s record so far shows the tenacity to achieve and accessibility to most effectively support our residents.

David works as a Management Consultant in the Telecoms Sector and brings this drive for efficiency to make a real difference and be able to deliver real value for your Council Tax.

Hi there, I’m Henry, your Green Party candidate for Wokingham Without. I’m hugely excited at the opportunity to bring fresh eyes and new perspectives to local issues, and the chance to serve our local community.

​All local issues are vitally important, though my personal relevant passions, knowledge and experiences mostly relate to appropriate and timely healthcare access alongside sustainable, safe and accessible transport for all residents. I’ve been a healthcare assistant in the NHS for five years, from outpatients to intensive care, and am intimately aware of the importance of community healthcare access, along with the passion of those who deliver it. I strongly believe supporting our health, plus the systems which deliver care, is the most fundamental duty of the council.

​Outside of healthcare, I’ve also worked as a British Cycling and Bikeability coach for schools and local bike clubs, plus as a local delegate for British Cycling National Council. Because of this, I am very familiar with the benefits of safe, accessible and sustainable transport options on health, and on personal and social mobility. Additionally, as a driver and a cyclist, I am acutely aware of what is and is not appropriate infrastructure. These are skills which I know can be utilised to make the most appropriate decisions on a local level.

​Every vote counts, and if elected I pledge to do my very best to serve Wokingham Without – thanks kindly!

I am running for councillor in the Wokingham Without ward. I am committed to investing in the green spaces and community life of the ward, ensuring that this continues to be a healthy and enriching place to live.
